title: Copy subitem data down, in a column, when creating new Subitem
excerpt:
In my Book Inventory Board, I have a subitem column to enter the name of a Book.  I have to enter this name everytime I enter a new subitem, so that it counts it in the inventory.  Any way to automate that? 
EXAMPLE:  Ma…

posts.author: Sta867 (Stacey White)
posts.cookedHtml:
In my Book Inventory Board, I have a subitem column to enter the name of a Book. I have to enter this name everytime I enter a new subitem, so that it counts it in the inventory. Any way to automate that?

EXAMPLE: Main Item name is: PAV-1

All subitems created under this main item, the Book Name column has to include PAV-1, otherwise it won’t count it for the inventory. Currently have to click on the cell and type it.

Thank you!!

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @Sta867 Yes, you can automate this

Best option: Use a Mirror column in the subitems to mirror the parent item name. This will automatically show “PAV-1” for every subitem and stay updated if the item name changes. Use this mirrored value for your inventory count.

Alternative: Create an automation:
“When a subitem is created → set Book Name to Item Name”

This auto-fills the Book Name every time, so you don’t have to type it manually.

Dr. Tanvi Sachar
Monday Certified Partner, Tuesday Wizard

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
This can be done pretty easily with the workflow center. Which product (CRM/Work Management, etc) and level (Pro/Enterprise) do you have?

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: Sta867 (Stacey White)
posts.cookedHtml:
So neither of these options were available to me. 1) When preparing a mirror column, “ITEM” isn’t available to choose to use as a mirror. 2) There is no automation available for set book name to item name (set something to something), only set date or set number.

I have Workspace management Pro. is that the issue? Are these not available to my subscription level?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is how you’d do it with the workflow center. Since you have Pro, you only get 5 ‘Active workflows’ though. If you’re open to 3rd party apps you might look at the app called ‘Subitem automations’.

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com
